arguments_for_delay_or_challenge: The packet describes a critical vulnerability allowing fee draining and XRP balance depletion.
Validators were explicitly advised to vote No.
The feature was disabled in client software and a replacement was planned, indicating the original amendment is obsolete/unsafe.

cited_facts.why_it_matters: Identifies a critical unsafe evidence pattern: a bug allowing unauthorized fee charging and balance draining.
Provides an explicit no-vote advisory from validators and confirms the feature was not enabled.
Confirms the amendment is effectively retired/obsolete as it was disabled and replaced.

output_json.decision_summary: The packet describes a critical vulnerability (fee drain) and includes an explicit no-vote advisory. The amendment was disabled and replaced. According to the precedence rules, REJECT is used for explicit no-vote advisories, retired/obsolete proposals, or critical unsafe evidence. All three conditions are met.
output_json.forbidden_stronger_claim: The amendment is safe to enable.

override_questions: Is there any evidence that the vulnerability has been patched in this specific amendment version?
validator_work_item.recommended_validator_action: Vote No to reject the amendment.
review_questions: Does the packet contain an explicit no-vote advisory?
Is the amendment described as containing critical unsafe evidence?
validator_work_item.title: Reject PermissionDelegation due to critical vulnerability and no-vote advisory
